title: map()
subtitle: Transform every element
goal: Learn to create a new array by transforming every element with map().
explanation: map() iterates over every element in an array.
A function runs for each element.
The results are collected in a new array.
The original array does not change.
map() is often used to prepare data.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a conveyor belt. Regular apples go in, and peeled apples come out the other side. Every element undergoes the same transformation.

remember: The original array does not change.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think map() changes the original array. In fact, it creates a new one.
